title: Skipping Steps Without Retraining
concept: DDIM can reuse a model trained with the standard DDPM objective but sample using far fewer steps (e.g. 50 instead of 1000) at inference time.

exercises.prompt: If a recipe has 1000 tiny cooking steps but you only have time for 50, what would you want to do?

options.text: Skip most steps but still follow the same recipe idea, just in bigger jumps
Throw away the recipe and cook randomly
Buy a completely different recipe book

exercises.explain: That's exactly the idea behind DDIM! You keep the same trained 'recipe' (model) but take bigger jumps instead of every tiny step.

cards.title: Why Wait 1000 Steps?
cards.body: Imagine a diffusion model that was trained to remove noise in 1000 tiny steps. What if you could get almost the same great result in just 50 steps, using the SAME trained model, no retraining needed? That's the magic trick DDIM brings to the table!

cards.title: What is DDIM?
cards.body: DDIM (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models) is a sampling method that takes a model trained the normal DDPM way (with 1000 small denoising steps) and generates images using far fewer steps, like 50, at inference time. The model itself doesn't change - only how we use it to sample changes.

cards.title: Taking the Elevator Instead of Stairs
cards.body: Imagine climbing 1000 stairs to reach the top of a building. DDPM walks every single stair. DDIM is like taking an elevator that skips floors, using the same building (same trained model) but reaching the top much faster with fewer stops.

cards.title: Fast Image Generation
cards.body: A model is trained using DDPM with 1000 steps to learn how to denoise images bit by bit. Later, instead of running all 1000 steps during generation, we use DDIM to sample with only 50 steps. The image still turns out clear and realistic, but it's generated 20x faster!

cards.title: Myth: You Need a New Model
cards.body: Myth: To sample faster, you must train a brand new special model. Truth: DDIM reuses the exact same model trained with the standard DDPM objective. No retraining needed - only the sampling process (how we use the model) changes!

cards.title: DDPM vs DDIM Sampling
cards.body: Both use the same trained model, but the sampling paths differ in how many steps they take to go from noise to image.

left.text: Takes all 1000 small steps, slow but steady denoising

right.text: Skips ahead using only ~50 steps, same model, much faster

exercises.prompt: What does DDIM allow you to do differently from standard DDPM sampling?

options.text: Use a completely different, newly trained model
Reuse the same trained model but sample with far fewer steps
Only work on text instead of images

exercises.explain: DDIM's key advantage is reusing the SAME model trained with the DDPM objective, just changing how sampling is done to use fewer steps.

exercises.prompt: True or False: DDIM requires retraining the diffusion model with a special new objective before you can sample faster.
exercises.explain: False! DDIM works with a model already trained the standard DDPM way - no retraining is required to enjoy faster sampling.

exercises.sentence: DDIM can sample using far fewer steps, like 50 instead of 1000, without needing to ___ the model.
exercises.explain: DDIM's superpower is skipping steps at sampling time without needing to retrain the underlying model.

exercises.prompt: A model was trained with 1000-step DDPM. At inference time, someone wants faster results. What should they do using DDIM?

options.text: Train a brand new model from scratch with fewer steps
Use the same trained model but sample with fewer steps, like 50
Increase the steps to 2000 for better quality

exercises.explain: DDIM lets you reuse the already-trained model and simply change the sampling process to use fewer steps, saving time.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best captures the core idea of DDIM sampling?

options.text: DDIM needs a special new training objective different from DDPM
DDIM reuses a standard DDPM-trained model but samples with far fewer steps at inference time
DDIM only works if you use exactly 1000 steps
DDIM replaces the neural network with a simpler one

exercises.explain: Correct! DDIM's key insight is that the same DDPM-trained model can be sampled with far fewer steps (e.g., 50 instead of 1000) without any retraining, making generation much faster.
